```js
await page.goto('https://example.com');
```
### Page
- Page URL: https://example.com/
- Page Title: Example Domain
### Snapshot

### Post-navigation snapshot
### Page
- Page URL: https://example.com/
- Page Title: Example Domain

```yaml
- generic [ref=e2]:
  - heading "Example Domain" [level=1] [ref=e3]
  - paragraph [ref=e4]: This domain is for use in documentation examples without needing permission. Avoid use in operations.
  - paragraph [ref=e5]:
    - link "Learn more" [ref=e6] [cursor=pointer]:
      - /url: https://iana.org/domains/example
```

guide.childGuide: Treat the user request as a single browser execution intent with one controlled chunk; do not broaden the task or add extra subtasks beyond navigation, screenshot capture, and title retrieval.

structuredOutput.llmIntentRationale: The user request is a procedural browser task with a clear completion condition and no need for decomposition beyond one chunk. The only detected fragment kind in the input is constraint, so the output preserves that controlled classification and avoids introducing additional fragment kinds.

dbagent.materializationRationale: Materialize the checked snapshot into differentiated DB-backed package families so the active context is served from a single explicit primary package, historical evolution is preserved in a separate timeline package, and each checked task context is reproducible via its own task snapshot. Parent-side promote requests should be reviewed through a dedicated parent-owned review entry rather than mutating child package state directly. Passive replies should be retained with high fidelity in passive-history storage and indexed from the timeline and task snapshots so historical responses can be replayed or served without lossy recomputation.
